artifact: REF-C-small (54.69M) 30k training run — closes the D-030 3-size scaling ladder
exact_command: cd /workspace/TanitAD/stack && PYTHONPATH=/workspace/TanitAD/stack PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True nohup python3 scripts/refc_train.py --data-root /workspace/data/physicalai_phase0/_epcache --out /workspace/experiments/refc-diffusion-small-v21-30k --steps 30000 --mode diffusion --config small --anchors /workspace/experiments/refc_anchors_small64.pt --batch 20 --workers 6 --labels v21
mirrors: refc-diffusion-base-v21-30k (registry Sec 4.3) exactly; deltas are ONLY --config small + 64-anchor vocab (holds --labels v21 constant so small-vs-base isolates SCALE)

anchors.derivation: base128[:64] == full256[:64] — bit-exact nested FPS prefix (seed 0). Guarantees the scale-A/B matched-vocabulary control (torch.equal check in refc_scale_ab.analyze) passes vs both base (128) and XL (256).
anchors.verified: torch.equal(small64, base128[:64]) AND torch.equal(base128, full256[:128]) AND torch.equal(small64, full256[:64])

parity_proofs.note_windows: matches registry Sec 0.1 (2376 eps / 406099 windows) — same parity set as base/XL
parity_proofs.note_labels: bit-identical to base run config.json (registry Sec 4.3: left 0.121 / straight 0.5645 / right 0.115 / UNKNOWN 0.1995 / 80.05% judgeable) — proves the v21 labeler on pod2's cache reproduces the base run's label distribution
parity_proofs.param_acceptance: refc_small_config() total == 54,690,001 (registry Sec 4.2) — proves refc.py on pod2 is current

eval_plan: on finish: taniteval.refc_eval (ADE/FDE/miss/strata, 40 canonical val eps) + refc_scale_ab dump+analyze vs refc-base-30k AND refc-xl-30k (oracle-in-fan, sel_gap, frac_sel_2x_worse, paired episode-cluster bootstrap via taniteval/ci.py). Persist windows_refc-small-30k.pt + fan tensor. Add registry.py entry.
decision_question: Does small (2.4x below base) HOLD the base-XL tie, or does the fan finally degrade? If oracle-in-fan jumps at small -> scale is a lever below base (ladder has a knee). If small also ties -> REF-C is absurdly over-parameterised and v4's decoder can shrink further.

completion.transfer: pod2->eval via HF relay (Sayood/tanitad-refc-small-evalonly, private) — model-only ckpt 219MB, md5 0e866fd85f9fc9399dd09f12bccefd0a verified both ends; scp -3 dev-box relay dropped at 72% first (too slow/flaky)
completion.eval_pod_state: arch-inf lock was STALE (pid gone), GPU free — no wait needed
completion.load_test: STRICT load PASS on eval refc.py (54,690,001 params, step 29999, anchors [64,4,2])

verdict: Ladder has a SMALL KNEE at the bottom rung: small is SEPARATED-worse than base AND XL on selected ADE@2s by ~0.053 m (base vs XL was NOT separated). BUT the knee is ANCHOR-COUNT (64 vs 128), not encoder scale: on the matched 64-anchor vocabulary small's fan BEATS base's and far beats XL's, and small's oracle is tightest at every shared K. The 2.4x encoder/param cut did not degrade proposal quality. Extends Sec 4.3 a rung down: the fan lever is anchor-vocabulary WIDTH, not encoder scale. => v4 can shrink the encoder toward ~48M with no fan-quality loss, provided the anchor vocab stays wide (>=128, ~free buffers).
evidence_class: MEASURED (this run + canonical eval; raw JSON in this dir + eval pod /root/taniteval/results/)
